summary: P0 REGRESSION FIX VERIFIED - Admin credit/debit transactions now render correctly in client transaction history with all professional banking metadata fields

hotfix_validation.original_bug: Admin-created credit/debit transactions displayed weird/inconsistent layout with missing metadata fields in client-side transaction history
hotfix_validation.root_cause: Admin topup/withdraw endpoints in accounts.py were NOT passing professional banking fields (display_type, sender_name, sender_iban, etc.) as metadata to ledger_engine.top_up() and ledger_engine.withdraw()
hotfix_validation.fix_applied: Added transaction_metadata object construction in admin_account_topup() and admin_account_withdraw() functions that includes all professional fields (display_type, sender_name, sender_iban, sender_bic, reference, description, admin_note for credits; display_type, recipient_name, to_iban, reference, description, admin_note for debits) and passes it to ledger_engine

key_validations: ✓ Admin credit endpoint stores display_type metadata
✓ Admin credit endpoint stores sender_name metadata
✓ Admin credit endpoint stores sender_iban metadata
✓ Admin credit endpoint stores sender_bic metadata
✓ Admin credit endpoint stores reference metadata
✓ Admin debit endpoint stores display_type metadata
✓ Admin debit endpoint stores recipient_name metadata
✓ Admin debit endpoint stores to_iban metadata
✓ Admin debit endpoint stores reference metadata
✓ Client can see all metadata fields when fetching transactions
✓ E2E credit flow: Admin creates → Client sees all metadata
✓ E2E debit flow: Admin creates → Client sees all metadata

results: ✓ Recent Activity section shows 5 transaction items
✓ Bank Transfer transaction shows 'From: ACME Corporation'
✓ Wire Transfer transaction shows 'From: E2E Test Corp'
✓ SEPA Transfer transactions show proper debit styling
✓ Recent Activity renders correctly on mobile (390x844)
✓ Credit badges (2) and Debit badges (3) visible
✓ Wire Transfer credit modal shows all professional fields
✓ SEPA Transfer debit modal shows all professional fields

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Backend accounts.py properly builds transaction_metadata with all professional fields
✓ None values filtered out from metadata to keep it clean
✓ Frontend ProfessionalDashboard.js correctly reads metadata fields
✓ isTransactionCredit() utility properly identifies credit/debit based on transaction_type and direction
✓ Transaction amount formatting uses proper EU format (€X.XXX,XX)
seed_data_creation: Created multiple test transactions via backend tests (E2E_TEST credits, E2E_DEBIT debits, TEST_REF credits/debits)
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 admin credit/debit metadata regression VERIFIED. Test user ashleyalt005@gmail.com has multiple test transactions showing the fix works. Credit transactions display sender info, IBAN, BIC, reference correctly. Debit transactions display recipient info, to_iban, reference correctly. Both desktop and mobile viewports render transactions properly.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Admin-created credit/debit transactions showed weird/inconsistent layout in client transaction history
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: admin_account_topup() and admin_account_withdraw() in /app/backend/routers/accounts.py were not passing professional banking fields as metadata to ledger_engine. The frontend ProfessionalDashboard.js expected these fields in txn.metadata for proper rendering.
reproduction_steps: 2. Admin creates credit (topup) with professional fields (sender_name, sender_iban, etc.)
3. Client logs in and views transaction history
4. BEFORE FIX: Transaction showed without sender info, weird layout
5. AFTER FIX: Transaction shows 'From: Sender Name', IBAN, BIC, reference properly
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Added transaction_metadata object construction in admin_account_topup() (lines 566-577) and admin_account_withdraw() (lines 631-642) that captures all professional fields and passes them to ledger_engine.top_up() and ledger_engine.withdraw() as the metadata parameter.
